div.div: A retro modifier in a DIFFERENT Consent resource has NO effect on .6 provisions from another resource.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 778px; height: 1px; padding-top: 600px; margin-left: 7px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Only retro modifier denies (.45/.46 deny) can reduce the retro-extended period — prospective denies (.6 deny) cannot.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 778px; height: 1px; padding-top: 620px; margin-left: 7px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-style: italic; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: .45/.46 only have this effect if explicitly requested alongside .6 in the CRTDL cohort definition — otherwise they are not evaluated at all.

a.text: Text is not SVG - cannot display
